skill.name: video-inpainting
skill.description: Region edits across video frames on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — remove an object that appears across many frames, clean up wires or watermarks, replace a region with matching motion. Routes across Wan 2-7 edit-video (default, prompt-driven region edits with spatial language), Lucy Edit Restyle (identity-stable region-aware restyle), and Seedream 4-0 edit-sequential (when treating the clip as a frame stack). Picks the right route based on whether the change is prose-driven, identity-locked, or needs frame-by-frame still inpaint chained into a video. Triggers on "video inpaint", "video inpainting", "remove from video", "mask region in video", "clean up video", "remove object from clip", "video patch", "frame-by-frame edit", "remove watermark from video", "remove passing person", or any explicit ask to edit a region across video frames.
skill.summary: Remove objects, watermarks, and unwanted regions from video frames using RunComfy AI models via the runcomfy CLI.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 76 potential issues across 3 risk categories (external_commands, network, filesystem). All findings are false positives in context: shell backtick commands document legitimate runcomfy CLI usage for video processing, hardcoded URLs reference the RunComfy service and documentation, and filesystem references describe standard token storage. The CRITICAL flag for 'Windows SAM database' is a false positive -- SAM2 refers to Meta's Segment Anything Model 2 (computer vision), not the Windows Security Account Manager. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: External command execution via shell backtick patterns
medium_findings.description: The skill executes shell commands using backtick syntax to invoke the runcomfy CLI for video processing. This is expected behavior for a CLI-wrapper skill and is properly scoped to Bash(runcomfy *) only. No command injection risk exists as arguments are passed as JSON strings.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded service URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains multiple hardcoded URLs pointing to runcomfy.com, skills.sh, and docs.runcomfy.com. These link to the RunComfy service homepage, model catalog, CLI documentation, and related skills. This is transparent and expected for a skill that wraps a cloud API service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell backtick command execution for CLI invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses shell backtick syntax to execute runcomfy CLI commands for video processing. Pattern: `runcomfy run <model> --input '<json>' --output-dir <dir>`. This is the intended mechanism of the skill and is properly bounded by the Bash(runcomfy *) tool restriction. Arguments are JSON-encoded and not subject to shell injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: API credential stored in filesystem
dangerous_patterns.description: The runcomfy login command stores an API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This is standard CLI tool behavior documented transparently by the skill. The skill advises mode 0600 permissions and environment variable alternatives for CI environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network communication with external API service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends video processing requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and related subdomains. This is the core function of the skill. The skill documents the outbound endpoint allowlist and a 2 GiB download cap.

content.user_title: Edit and restore video regions with AI inpainting
content.value_statement: Remove unwanted objects, watermarks, and people from video frames automatically. This skill routes your editing request to the best RunComfy AI model for region-aware video inpainting without requiring manual frame-by-frame work.
actual_capabilities: Remove watermarks and logos across all video frames using spatial language prompts
Replace specific objects or regions with motion-matched content that follows the scene
Route editing requests to the optimal RunComfy model based on editing intent
Support prompt-driven region edits without requiring explicit pixel masks
Provide access to ComfyUI workflows for pixel-precise mask propagation when needed
Handle sequential frame-by-frame processing for frame-stack editing
limitations: Cannot perform pixel-precise mask-driven edits through the CLI alone
Temporal consistency may degrade for long clips and complex motion
Requires an active RunComfy account and API token
Compound edits with multiple changes in one pass may produce unreliable results

use_cases.title: Remove watermarks from video content
use_cases.description: Editors can remove logos, timestamps, and watermarks from video clips by describing the target region in plain language.

use_cases.title: Clean up unwanted objects in footage
use_cases.description: Remove passing people, cables, debris, or other distracting elements from video frames without manual frame-by-frame editing.

use_cases.title: Replace regions in video with AI-generated content
use_cases.description: Swap out specific areas like skies, signs, or product placements with new content that matches the scene motion and lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic watermark removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner across all video frames. Preserve all other content exactly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Removing a static watermark or logo from a video clip

prompt_templates.title: Object removal with spatial language
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the person walking in the background wearing a red shirt. Fill the area with matching background that follows the scene motion.
prompt_templates.scenario: Removing a passing person from video footage

prompt_templates.title: Region replacement with preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the sky in this video with a sunset gradient. Keep the foreground and all objects exactly the same. Match lighting and exposure across frames.
prompt_templates.scenario: Changing visual elements like sky or background in a video clip

prompt_templates.title: Sequential frame-by-frame edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Process each frame of this video clip individually. Apply the same edit instruction across every frame: remove the cable that appears in the top-left area. Re-encode the edited frames back into a video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Removing an object that requires frame-by-frame treatment for precision

output_examples.input: Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner of this video.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request through Wan 2-7 Edit-Video and removes the watermark region. The output is a clean video where the watermark area is filled with content that matches the surrounding frames in motion and color.

output_examples.input: Remove the person crossing the frame in the background of this clip.
output_examples.output: The skill detects the passing person across frames and removes them. The background is reconstructed to follow the natural scene motion. For better results with complex motion, the skill can recommend a ComfyUI workflow with SAM2 segmentation tracking.

best_practices: Always describe the target region using spatial language such as bottom-right corner or the area behind the main subject.
Include preservation language like Preserve all other content exactly to prevent unintended changes to other parts of the frame.
Make one edit per pass. Combining multiple changes in a single request can cause visual drift and unreliable results.
anti_patterns: Requesting multiple simultaneous edits like remove object A and replace object B. Split complex edits into sequential passes.
Using vague spatial descriptions without specifying which region to target. Be precise about location.
Expecting pixel-perfect mask precision from prompt-only models. Use ComfyUI workflows when surgical accuracy is required.

faq.question: What video formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with video URLs that you provide. The RunComfy CLI downloads and processes the video from your specified URL.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Install the CLI with npm i -g @runcomfy/cli, then sign in with runcomfy login. You need an active account and API token.

faq.question: Can this skill remove any object from a video?
faq.answer: It can remove objects that you describe in spatial language. Results depend on object size, motion complexity, and scene context.

faq.question: How long does video inpainting take?
faq.answer: Processing time depends on video length, resolution, and model queue. The CLI polls for completion and downloads the result automatically.

faq.question: What is the difference between Wan 2-7 and Seedream models?
faq.answer: Wan 2-7 Edit-Video handles prompt-driven region edits across frames. Seedream 4-0 Edit-Sequential treats video as individual frames for frame-by-frame processing.

faq.question: Is my video data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes. The video is processed on RunComfy cloud servers. Only outbound endpoints model-api.runcomfy.net and subdomains are used.
